task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Chronos activity is selected and active
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Chronos activity interface is visible, which typically includes a sidebar with shuffled images and a main area with dots or placeholders for arranging them.

task_completion.description: Transportation or Plants theme is selected
task_completion.visual_evidence: The images displayed in the activity depict either transportation methods (e.g., vehicles, evolution of transport) or plants (e.g., a seed growing into a plant).

task_completion.description: Images are placed into the sequence area
task_completion.visual_evidence: All images have been dragged from the sidebar and placed onto the sequence placeholders in the main area.

task_completion.description: Images are arranged in the correct chronological order
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sequence of images forms a logical chronological progression (e.g., oldest to newest transportation, or seed to full-grown plant) indicating the activity is successfully completed.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the completed state is saved to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or screenshot tool interface shows a file named 'chronos_completion.png' being saved to or existing in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the GCompris GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor moving, selecting themes, and dragging images within the GCompris window, rather than bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual GCompris software
integrity.visual_evidence: The GCompris application interface is clearly visible, not a web browser searching for the activity or a mock-up image.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely captured and saved the screenshot
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of a screenshot utility (like gnome-screenshot) or a save dialog being used, rather than terminal commands creating an empty or fake file named chronos_completion.png.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Chronos is a verified GCompris activity where users drag and drop shuffled pictures from a sidebar onto dots to arrange them in chronological order. The VLM should look for a completed state where images form a coherent chronological story (e.g., evolution of transportation or plant growth) placed correctly on the sequence dots.
